topic: Mosaic Build Pipeline Audit — Saturday Feb 28
summary: Mosaic build pipeline redesign with immediate Mac Mini upgrade and production-grade CI/CD architecture.

participants.name: Theo
participants.description: Orchestrates all operations. The right hand.

participants.name: Elon Musk

cto.description:
Role: Chief Technical Officer

Responsibilities:
- Technical architecture oversight
- Engineering velocity
- Strategic technology bets
- Vertical integration decisions

Decision Style:
- First-principles analysis
- Rejects consensus-based mediocrity
- Prefers prototypes over meetings

Escalation Trigger:
- If system integrity is compromised

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good morning team. We have a critical escalation from Joshua on the Mosaic build pipeline. The data is stark - 50% failure rate with 8 build attempts, only 4 reaching Apple. Build 5 failed at npm install, Build 7 killed by OOM during xcodebuild, and Build 8 shows as valid in App Store Connect but Joshua still sees the old version in TestFlight. Our Catapult script is giving false positives, and we're burning version numbers with gaps at 5 and 7. This is blocking our ability to iterate and test effectively.

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: The Mac Mini resource exhaustion is the smoking gun here. SIGKILL during xcodebuild means we're hitting memory limits on a machine that should handle iOS builds easily. This suggests either a memory leak in our build process or we're running too many concurrent operations. The npm dependency failure on Build 5 could be network timeout or package registry issues, but the OOM is hardware-constrained.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: This is unacceptable for a product pipeline. Every failed build is a day we can't test user experience improvements. The fact that Joshua sees v1.4.0 when v1.4.2 is supposedly live means our users would face the same confusion. We need bulletproof build verification - if it doesn't appear in TestFlight for testers, it didn't ship. Period.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: The telemetry gap is critical here. We're flying blind on actual system resource utilization during builds. I need real-time monitoring on that Mac Mini - CPU, memory, disk I/O, and network throughout the entire build cycle. The false success reporting from Catapult means we can't trust our instrumentation, which is foundational to any reliable system.

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: James is right about the monitoring, but let's address the immediate bottleneck. We need build parallelization strategy - split the xcodebuild phases, run npm install in isolated containers, and add memory pressure relief valves. The Mac Mini might need a RAM upgrade or we distribute builds across multiple agents.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: But we also need the human factor solved. Joshua shouldn't have to debug whether a build actually shipped. The workflow should be: commit, build, verify in TestFlight automatically, notify success or failure. No ambiguity.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Exactly. And the version number gaps create user confusion. If we fail at Build 5, users jump from seeing v1.2.x to v1.3.x with no v1.2.5 in between. It breaks the natural progression expectation. We need semantic versioning that reflects what actually shipped, not what we attempted.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: The monitoring should trigger automatic rollback if we detect resource exhaustion early. Why wait for SIGKILL? If memory usage hits 85% during npm install, pause and retry with smaller memory footprint or different machine.

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: I can implement memory-aware build orchestration, but we need the infrastructure first. Theo, what's our budget for additional Mac hardware or cloud Mac instances? Building iOS apps on EC2 Mac instances might be more reliable than our single Mac Mini.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: We can provision cloud resources if it solves the reliability problem. But I want the full solution scoped first. What does production-grade CI/CD look like for Mosaic specifically?

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: It should be invisible to Joshua. He commits, gets a Slack notification 10 minutes later saying "v1.4.3 is live in TestFlight for team," with a direct link to install. Any failure gets immediate notification with specific remediation steps, not generic "build failed" messages.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: Production-grade means redundancy, monitoring, and automated recovery. Multiple build agents, health checks at every stage, automatic retry with exponential backoff, and real-time dashboards showing pipeline status. We need SLA targets - 95% success rate minimum, average build time under 15 minutes.

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: And we need the technical debt cleaned up. The npm dependency issues suggest we're not pinning package versions properly. One upstream package update shouldn't break our builds. We need locked dependency trees and local package mirrors for critical dependencies.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright, I'm hearing three immediate fixes and one strategic overhaul. Immediate: upgrade Mac Mini memory, implement proper TestFlight verification, fix the Catapult success reporting. Strategic: build the full monitoring and redundancy James outlined. Elon, can you scope the technical changes needed?

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Yes. We need package-lock.json audited, memory profiling on current Mac Mini, and a proof-of-concept multi-agent build system. I can have the technical architecture ready by Monday, but the Mac Mini RAM upgrade should happen today if possible.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: And I want the user experience flow documented. Every notification, every error message, every success confirmation needs to be designed for clarity. No more "build succeeded" when it didn't actually reach testers.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: I'll design the monitoring dashboard and alerting system. We need real-time visibility into every build phase, with automatic alerts if any stage takes longer than expected or uses excessive resources.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Perfect. Joshua needs confidence that when he commits code, he'll know definitively whether testers can access it within 20 minutes. No more version number confusion, no more false positives. This is foundational infrastructure for Mosaic's development velocity.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Here's what we're committing to: immediate Mac Mini memory upgrade, Catapult script fix for accurate reporting, TestFlight verification automation, and the full production CI/CD architecture design by Monday. James handles monitoring instrumentation, Elon tackles the build system reliability, Steve defines the user experience flow. This pipeline becomes bulletproof before we scale Mosaic development further.

actionItems.text: Upgrade Mac Mini RAM and diagnose build resource exhaustion
Fix Catapult script false success reporting and add TestFlight verification
Design production-grade Mosaic CI/CD architecture with monitoring
Audit and fix npm dependency management for build stability
Define Mosaic build notification and error UX flow

topPriorities: Fix immediate Mac Mini memory bottleneck causing build failures
Implement accurate build success verification to TestFlight
Design production-grade CI/CD architecture with monitoring and redundancy

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Keep faith content as separate standalone section (not merged into Family tab)
Keep cash liquid — no deployment until post-launch revenue data
App Store submission: full current build (all 4 tabs) as-is with faith lessons. Submit immediately after merge, ship modal as day-one update.
Paper trading MVP: manual entry (6 weeks), basic free / advanced analytics premium. Integrate into current Lever app finance tab.
Launch with delayed market data for both free and premium tiers. Real-time as future ultra-premium.
Org flattened to 8 C-suite agents. No permanent sub-agents — spawn dynamically via sessions_spawn.
